1	# Copyright (c) godot-rust; Bromeon and contributors.

4	# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.
5	
6	name: Full CI
7	#
8	# Runs before merging. Rebases on master to make sure CI passes for latest integration, not only for the PR at the time of creation.
9	
10	on:
11	  # Allow manually triggering the workflow:
12	  # https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/choosing-when-your-workflow-runs/events-that-trigger-workflows#workflow_dispatch
13	  workflow_dispatch:
14	  merge_group:
15	#  push:
16	
17	
18	env:
19	  # Applies to all 'register-docs' features across crates.
20	  CLIPPY_FEATURES: '--features register-docs,godot/experimental-godot-api,godot/serde'
21	  TEST_FEATURES: ''
22	  RETRY: ${{ github.workspace }}/.github/other/retry.sh
23	
24	  # ASan options: https://github.com/google/sanitizers/wiki/AddressSanitizerFlags
25	  # LSan options: https://github.com/google/sanitizers/wiki/AddressSanitizerLeakSanitizer
26	  # * report_objects: list individual leaked objects when running LeakSanitizer
27	  LSAN_OPTIONS: report_objects=1
28	
29	  CARGO_DENY_VERSION: "0.18.9"
30	  CARGO_MACHETE_VERSION: "0.9.1"
31	
32	
33	defaults:
34	  run:
35	    shell: bash
36	
37	# If a new commit is pushed before the old one's CI has completed (on the same branch), abort previous run
38	#concurrency:
39	#  group: ${{ github.head_ref }}
40	#  cancel-in-progress: true
41	
42	jobs:
43	  # Keep all in sync with minimal-ci and release-version.
44	  rustfmt:
45	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
46	    steps:
47	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
48	
49	      - name: "Install Rust"
50	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
51	        with:
52	          rust: nightly
53	          components: rustfmt
54	
55	      - name: "Check rustfmt"
56	        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check
57	
58	      - name: "Run custom repo checks"
59	        run: |
60	          cargo run -p repo-tweak
61	          git diff --quiet --exit-code || {
62	            echo "::error::Godot versions out of sync; update with `cargo run -p repo-tweak`."
63	            echo "Differences:"
64	            echo "----------------------------------------------------"
65	            git diff
66	            echo "----------------------------------------------------"
67	            exit 1
68	          }
69	
70	
71	  # Needs to be its own job (apart from sync-doc), because lints don't work with --no-deps, and because it contributes to ci-status.
72	  doc-lints:
73	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
74	    steps:
75	    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
76	
77	    - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
78	      run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly
79	
80	    # rustdoc is included as component.
81	    - name: "Install Rust"
82	      uses: ./.github/composite/rust
83	
84	    - name: "Check rustdoc"
85	      env:
86	        RUSTDOCFLAGS: >
87	          -D rustdoc::broken-intra-doc-links -D rustdoc::private-intra-doc-links -D rustdoc::invalid-codeblock-attributes
88	          -D rustdoc::invalid-rust-codeblocks -D rustdoc::invalid-html-tags -D rustdoc::bare-urls -D rustdoc::unescaped-backticks
89	          -D warnings
90	      run: cargo doc -p godot --ignore-rust-version
91	
92	
93	  clippy:
94	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
95	    steps:
96	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
97	
98	      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
99	        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly
100	
101	      - name: "Install Rust"
102	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
103	        with:
104	          components: clippy
105	
106	      # Note: could use `-- --no-deps` to not lint dependencies, however it doesn't really speed up and also skips deps in workspace.
107	      - name: "Check clippy"
108	        run: |
109	          cargo clippy --all-targets $CLIPPY_FEATURES -- \
110	          -D clippy::suspicious \
111	          -D clippy::style \
112	          -D clippy::complexity \
113	          -D clippy::perf \
114	          -D clippy::dbg_macro \
115	          -D clippy::todo \
116	          -D clippy::unimplemented \
117	          -D warnings
118	
119	  unit-test:
120	    name: unit-test (${{ matrix.name }}${{ matrix.rust-special }})
121	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
122	    continue-on-error: false
123	    strategy:
124	      fail-fast: false # cancel all jobs as soon as one fails?
125	      matrix:
126	        # Order this way because macOS typically has the longest duration, followed by Windows, so it benefits total workflow execution time.
127	        # Additionally, the 'linux (msrv *)' special case will then be listed next to the other 'linux' jobs.
128	        # Note: Windows uses '--target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc' by default as Cargo argument.
129	        include:
130	          - name: macos
131	            os: macos-latest # arm64
132	
133	          - name: windows
134	            os: windows-latest
135	
136	          # Don't use latest Ubuntu (22.04) as it breaks lots of ecosystem compatibility.
137	          # If ever moving to ubuntu-latest, need to manually install libtinfo5 for LLVM.
138	          - name: linux
139	            os: ubuntu-22.04
140	
141	          - name: linux
142	            os: ubuntu-22.04
143	            rust-toolchain: nightly
144	            rust-special: -minimal-deps
145	            rust-cache-key: minimal-deps
146	
147	          - name: linux
148	            os: ubuntu-22.04
149	            rust-toolchain: "1.87"
150	            rust-special: -msrv
151	
152	    steps:
153	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
154	
155	      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
156	        # Only on Linux because godot4-prebuilt/nightly branch doesn't have artifacts for other platforms.
157	        if: matrix.name == 'linux' && matrix.rust-special == ''
158	        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly
159	
160	      - name: "Install Rust"
161	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
162	        with:
163	          rust: ${{ matrix.rust-toolchain || 'stable' }}
164	          cache-key: ${{ matrix.rust-cache-key }} # only needed when rustc version is possibly the same
165	
166	      # Set Cargo features also to minimal; detects when doctests refer to API symbols outside the minimal codegen set.
167	      - name: "Install minimal dependency versions from Cargo"
168	        if: matrix.rust-special == '-minimal-deps'
169	        run: |
170	          cargo +nightly update -Z minimal-versions
171	          echo "TEST_FEATURES=--no-default-features" >> $GITHUB_ENV
172	
173	      - name: "Compile tests"
174	        run: cargo test $TEST_FEATURES --no-run ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}
175	
176	      - name: "Test"
177	        run: cargo test $TEST_FEATURES ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}
178	
179	
180	  unit-test-web:
181	    name: unit-test-web (${{ matrix.name }})
182	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
183	    strategy:
184	      fail-fast: false
185	      matrix:
186	        include:
187	          - name: Threads
188	            command: test-web-t
189	          - name: No threads
190	            command: test-web-nt
191	
192	    steps:
193	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
194	
195	      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
196	        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly
197	
198	      - name: "Install Rust"
199	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
200	        with:
201	          rust: nightly
202	          components: rust-src
203	
204	      - name: "Install emscripten"
205	        env:
206	          EMSCRIPTEN_SRC_REF: 4.0.13
207	          EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION: 3.1.74
208	        run: |
209	          git clone https://github.com/emscripten-core/emsdk.git --depth 1 --branch "$EMSCRIPTEN_SRC_REF" --single-branch
210	          ./emsdk/emsdk install "$EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION"
211	          ./emsdk/emsdk activate "$EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION"
212	          source ./emsdk/emsdk_env.sh
213	          echo "$PATH" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"
214	
215	      - name: "Test (Wasm)"
216	        run: ./check.sh ${{ matrix.command }}
217	
218	
219	  miri-test:
220	    name: miri-test
221	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
222	    steps:
223	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
224	
225	      - name: "Install Rust"
226	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
227	        with:
228	          rust: nightly
229	          components: miri
230	
231	      - name: "Setup Miri"
232	        run: cargo miri setup
233	
234	      - name: "Compile tests"
235	        run: cargo miri test -p godot-cell --no-run
236	
237	      - name: "Test stacked borrows"
238	        run: cargo miri test -p godot-cell
239	
240	      - name: "Test tree borrows"
241	        run: MIRIFLAGS="-Zmiri-tree-borrows" cargo miri test -p godot-cell
242	
243	  proptest:
244	    name: proptest
245	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
246	    steps:
247	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
248	
249	      - name: "Install Rust"
250	        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
251	
252	      - name: "Compile tests"
253	        run: cargo test -p godot-cell --features="proptest" --no-run
254	
255	      - name: "Test"
256	        run: cargo test -p godot-cell --features="proptest"
257	
258	  # For complex matrix workflow, see https://stackoverflow.com/a/65434401
259	  godot-itest:
260	    name: godot-itest (${{ matrix.name }})
261	    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
262	    continue-on-error: false
263	    timeout-minutes: 24
264	    strategy:
265	      fail-fast: false # cancel all jobs as soon as one fails?
266	      matrix:
267	        # Naming: {os}[-{runtimeVersion}]-{apiVersion}
268	        # runtimeVersion = version of Godot binary; apiVersion = version of GDExtension API against which gdext is compiled.
269	
270	        # Config overview (see job for details).
271	        #
272	        # Cross-platform:
273	        # - Base version:     Godot nightly, no custom
274	        # - Double precision: Godot nightly, custom (bindgen), double
275	        # - 4.2 compat:       Godot 4.2
276	        #
277	        # Linux-only:
278	        # - Full:             Godot nightly, full codegen
279	        # - Double + lazy:    Godot nightly, custom, double, lazy func tables
280	        # - Features + exp:   Godot nightly, custom, threads, serde, experimental API
281	        # - Memcheck nightly: Godot mem nightly, custom, sanitizer
282	        # - Memcheck 4.x:     Godot mem 4.2, sanitizer
283	
284	        # Note: Windows uses '--target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc' by default as Cargo argument.
285	        include:
286	          # macOS -- for the unintuitive naming, see https://github.com/actions/runner-images?tab=readme-ov-file#available-images
287	
288	          - name: macos-arm
289	            os: macos-latest
290	            artifact-name: macos-arm-nightly
291	            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64
292	            hot-reload: stable
293	
294	           # api-custom on macOS arm64 not working, due to clang linker issues.
295	#          - name: macos-double-arm
296	#            os: macos-latest
297	#            artifact-name: macos-double-arm-nightly
298	#            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.double.arm64
299	#            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision
300	
301	          # Lag one version behind latest release.
302	          - name: macos-arm-4.5
303	            os: macos-latest
304	            artifact-name: macos-arm-4.5
305	            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64
306	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'
307	
308	          # Windows
309	
310	          - name: windows
311	            os: windows-latest
312	            artifact-name: windows-nightly
313	            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.x86_64.exe
314	            hot-reload: stable
315	
316	          - name: windows-double
317	            os: windows-latest
318	            artifact-name: windows-double-nightly
319	            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.double.x86_64.exe
320	            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision
321	
322	          # Lag one version behind latest release.
323	          - name: windows-4.5
324	            os: windows-latest
325	            artifact-name: windows-4.5
326	            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.x86_64.exe
327	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'
328	
329	          # Linux
330	
331	          # Don't use latest Ubuntu (22.04) as it breaks lots of ecosystem compatibility.
332	          # If ever moving to ubuntu-latest, need to manually install libtinfo5 for LLVM.
333	
334	          # Uses full+experimental codegen, so that compatibility breakage towards nightly is detected.
335	          # If the experimental part causes problems, consider using only godot/__codegen-full.
336	          - name: linux-full
337	            os: ubuntu-22.04
338	            artifact-name: linux-nightly
339	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
340	            rust-extra-args: --features itest/codegen-full,itest/test-gdextension-dependency
341	            rust-extra-env: GODOT_RUST_MAIN_EXTENSION=IntegrationTests
342	            hot-reload: api-custom
343	
344	          # Combines now a lot of features, but should be OK. lazy-function-tables doesn't work with experimental-threads.
345	          - name: linux-double-lazy
346	            os: ubuntu-22.04
347	            artifact-name: linux-double-nightly
348	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.double.x86_64
349	            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision,itest/codegen-full,godot/lazy-function-tables
350	
351	          - name: linux-features-experimental
352	            os: ubuntu-22.04
353	            artifact-name: linux-nightly
354	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
355	            # Important to keep both experimental-threads and codegen-full. Some itests (native_st_audio) require both.
356	            rust-extra-args: --features itest/experimental-threads,itest/codegen-full-experimental,godot/api-custom,godot/serde,itest/register-docs
357	
358	          # Compiles godot-rust with `api-custom-json` feature against the JSON file generated via `--dump-extension-api`.
359	          # Uses latest 4.x headers, while `extension_api.json` comes from the latest Godot binary.
360	          - name: linux-custom-api-json
361	            os: ubuntu-22.04
362	            artifact-name: linux-nightly
363	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
364	            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom-json
365	            godot-indirect-json: true
366	
367	          - name: linux-release
368	            os: ubuntu-22.04
369	            artifact-name: linux-release-nightly
370	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.template_release.x86_64
371	            # Use `codegen-full-experimental` to make sure that all function tables can be loaded in Godot release builds.
372	            # If the experimental part causes problems, downgrade to `codegen-full`.
373	            rust-extra-args: --release --features itest/codegen-full-experimental
374	            rust-cache-key: release
375	
376	          # Linux compat:
377	          # No hot-reload before 4.4, as the Godot project is 4.4+.
378	
379	          - name: linux-4.6
380	            os: ubuntu-22.04
381	            artifact-name: linux-4.6
382	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
383	            #godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.6'
384	            hot-reload: stable
385	
386	          - name: linux-4.5
387	            os: ubuntu-22.04
388	            artifact-name: linux-4.5
389	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
390	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'
391	            hot-reload: api-4-5
392	
393	          - name: linux-4.4
394	            os: ubuntu-22.04
395	            artifact-name: linux-4.4
396	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
397	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.4'
398	            hot-reload: api-4-4
399	
400	          - name: linux-4.3
401	            os: ubuntu-22.04
402	            artifact-name: linux-4.3
403	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
404	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.3'
405	            hot-reload: api-4-3
406	
407	          # Can be disabled in the future (already covered by memcheck). For now kept on to differentiate quicker.
408	          - name: linux-4.2
409	            os: ubuntu-22.04
410	            artifact-name: linux-4.2
411	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
412	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.2'
413	            hot-reload: api-4-2
414	
415	          # Memory checks: special Godot binaries compiled with AddressSanitizer/LeakSanitizer to detect UB/leaks. Always Linux.
416	          # See also https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/sanitizers.html.
417	          #
418	          # Additionally, the Godot source is patched to make dlclose() a no-op, as unloading dynamic libraries loses stacktrace and
419	          # cause false positives like println!. See https://github.com/google/sanitizers/issues/89.
420	          #
421	          # There is also a gcc variant besides clang, which is currently not used.
422	          - name: memcheck-nightly
423	            os: ubuntu-22.04
424	            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
425	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
426	            rust-toolchain: nightly
427	            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
428	            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom
429	            # Sanitizers can't build proc-macros and build scripts; with --target, cargo ignores RUSTFLAGS for those two.
430	            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
431	
432	          - name: memcheck-release-disengaged
433	            os: ubuntu-22.04
434	            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
435	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
436	            rust-toolchain: nightly
437	            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
438	            # Currently without itest/codegen-full-experimental.
439	            rust-extra-args: --release --features godot/safeguards-release-disengaged
440	            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
441	            rust-target-dir: release # We're running Godot debug build with Rust release dylib.
442	
443	          - name: memcheck-dev-balanced
444	            os: ubuntu-22.04
445	            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
446	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
447	            rust-toolchain: nightly
448	            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
449	            # Currently without itest/codegen-full-experimental.
450	            rust-extra-args: --features godot/safeguards-dev-balanced
451	            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
452	
453	          - name: memcheck-4.2
454	            os: ubuntu-22.04
455	            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-4.2
456	            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
457	            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.2' # check compat of API 4.2 with newer binaries.
458	            rust-toolchain: nightly
459	            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
460	            # Sanitizers can't build proc-macros and build scripts; with --target, cargo ignores RUSTFLAGS for those two.
461	            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
462	
463	    steps:
464	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
465	
466	      - name: "Run Godot integration test"
467	        uses: ./.github/composite/godot-itest
468	        with:
469	          artifact-name: godot-${{ matrix.artifact-name }}
470	          godot-binary: ${{ matrix.godot-binary }}
471	          godot-args: ${{ matrix.godot-args }} # currently unused
472	          godot-prebuilt-patch: ${{ matrix.godot-prebuilt-patch }}
473	          rust-extra-args: ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}
474	          rust-toolchain: ${{ matrix.rust-toolchain || 'stable' }}
475	          rust-env-rustflags: ${{ matrix.rust-env-rustflags }}
476	          rust-extra-env: ${{ matrix.rust-extra-env }}
477	          rust-target: ${{ matrix.rust-target }}
478	          rust-target-dir: ${{ matrix.rust-target-dir }}
479	          rust-cache-key: ${{ matrix.rust-cache-key }}
480	          with-llvm: ${{ contains(matrix.name, 'macos') && contains(matrix.rust-extra-args, 'api-custom') }}
481	          godot-check-header: ${{ matrix.godot-check-header }}
482	          godot-indirect-json: ${{ matrix.godot-indirect-json }}
483	
484	      - name: "Build and test hot-reload"
485	        if: ${{ matrix.hot-reload }}
486	        working-directory: itest/hot-reload/godot
487	        # Repeat a few times, our hot reload integration test can sometimes be a bit flaky.
488	        # Don't pass in rust-extra-args as environment; they're intended for itest.
489	        run: $RETRY ./run-test.sh ${{ matrix.hot-reload }}
490	        shell: bash
491	
492	
493	  cargo-deny-machete:
494	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
495	    steps:
496	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
497	
498	      # Deny
499	      # Note: manually downloading is ~30s faster than https://github.com/EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny-action
500	      - name: "Install cargo-deny"
501	        run: |
502	          wget --no-verbose https://github.com/EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny/releases/download/$CARGO_DENY_VERSION/cargo-deny-$CARGO_DENY_VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz -O cargo-deny.tar.gz
503	          tar -zxvf cargo-deny.tar.gz
504	          mkdir -p $HOME/.cargo/bin
505	          mv cargo-deny-$CARGO_DENY_VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/cargo-deny $HOME/.cargo/bin
506	      - name: "Deny non-conforming dependencies"
507	        run: cargo deny check --config .github/other/deny.toml
508	
509	      # Machete
510	      - name: "Install cargo-machete"
511	        uses: baptiste0928/cargo-install@v3
512	        with:
513	          crate: cargo-machete
514	          version: ${{ env.CARGO_MACHETE_VERSION }}
515	      - name: "Use machete to cut down dependencies"
516	        run: cargo machete
517	
518	
519	  license-guard:
520	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
521	    steps:
522	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
523	
524	      - name: "Check license headers"
525	        uses: 'apache/skywalking-eyes/header@v0.6.0'
526	        with:
527	          # log: debug # optional: set the log level. The default value is `info`.
528	          config: .github/other/licenserc.yml
529	          # token: # optional: the token that license eye uses when it needs to comment on the pull request.
530	          # Set to empty ("") to disable commenting on pull request. The default value is ${{ github.token }}
531	          # mode: # optional: Which mode License-Eye should be run in. Choices are `check` or `fix`. The default value is `check`.
532	          mode: check
533	
534	
535	  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
536	  # CI status report
537	
538	  # Job to notify merge queue about success/failure
539	  ci-status:
540	    # Check for 'merge_group' not strictly necessary, but helpful when adding add-hoc `push:` trigger to `on:` for testing branch.
541	    if: always() && github.event_name == 'merge_group'
542	    needs:
543	      - rustfmt
544	      - doc-lints
545	      - clippy
546	      - unit-test
547	      - miri-test
548	      - proptest
549	      - godot-itest
550	      - cargo-deny-machete
551	      - license-guard
552	
553	    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
554	    steps:
555	      # Deliberate choice to use bash script and not GitHub Action glob syntax, as that is not well-documented and hard to get right.
556	      # For example: contains(needs.*.result, 'success') does NOT work because * is a logical OR, thus true if a single job succeeds.
557	      - name: "Determine success or failure"
558	        run: |
559	          DEPENDENCIES='${{ toJson(needs) }}'
560	
561	          echo "Dependency jobs:"
562	          all_success=true
563	          for job in $(echo "$DEPENDENCIES" | jq -r 'keys[]'); do
564	              status=$(echo "$DEPENDENCIES" | jq -r ".[\"$job\"].result")
565	              echo "* $job -> $status"
566	              if [[ "$status" != "success" ]]; then
567	                  all_success=false
568	              fi
569	          done
570	
571	          if [[ "$all_success" == "false" ]]; then
572	              echo "One or more dependency jobs failed or were cancelled."
573	              exit 1
574	          fi
575

# Copyright (c) godot-rust; Bromeon and contributors.
# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

name: Full CI
#
# Runs before merging. Rebases on master to make sure CI passes for latest integration, not only for the PR at the time of creation.

on:
  # Allow manually triggering the workflow:
  # https://docs.github.com/en/actions/writing-workflows/choosing-when-your-workflow-runs/events-that-trigger-workflows#workflow_dispatch
  workflow_dispatch:
  merge_group:
#  push:


env:
  # Applies to all 'register-docs' features across crates.
  CLIPPY_FEATURES: '--features register-docs,godot/experimental-godot-api,godot/serde'
  TEST_FEATURES: ''
  RETRY: ${{ github.workspace }}/.github/other/retry.sh

  # ASan options: https://github.com/google/sanitizers/wiki/AddressSanitizerFlags
  # LSan options: https://github.com/google/sanitizers/wiki/AddressSanitizerLeakSanitizer
  # * report_objects: list individual leaked objects when running LeakSanitizer
  LSAN_OPTIONS: report_objects=1

  CARGO_DENY_VERSION: "0.18.9"
  CARGO_MACHETE_VERSION: "0.9.1"


defaults:
  run:
    shell: bash

# If a new commit is pushed before the old one's CI has completed (on the same branch), abort previous run
#concurrency:
#  group: ${{ github.head_ref }}
#  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  # Keep all in sync with minimal-ci and release-version.
  rustfmt:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
        with:
          rust: nightly
          components: rustfmt

      - name: "Check rustfmt"
        run: cargo fmt --all -- --check

      - name: "Run custom repo checks"
        run: |
          cargo run -p repo-tweak
          git diff --quiet --exit-code || {
            echo "::error::Godot versions out of sync; update with `cargo run -p repo-tweak`."
            echo "Differences:"
            echo "----------------------------------------------------"
            git diff
            echo "----------------------------------------------------"
            exit 1
          }


  # Needs to be its own job (apart from sync-doc), because lints don't work with --no-deps, and because it contributes to ci-status.
  doc-lints:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4

    - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
      run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly

    # rustdoc is included as component.
    - name: "Install Rust"
      uses: ./.github/composite/rust

    - name: "Check rustdoc"
      env:
        RUSTDOCFLAGS: >
          -D rustdoc::broken-intra-doc-links -D rustdoc::private-intra-doc-links -D rustdoc::invalid-codeblock-attributes
          -D rustdoc::invalid-rust-codeblocks -D rustdoc::invalid-html-tags -D rustdoc::bare-urls -D rustdoc::unescaped-backticks
          -D warnings
      run: cargo doc -p godot --ignore-rust-version


  clippy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
        with:
          components: clippy

      # Note: could use `-- --no-deps` to not lint dependencies, however it doesn't really speed up and also skips deps in workspace.
      - name: "Check clippy"
        run: |
          cargo clippy --all-targets $CLIPPY_FEATURES -- \
          -D clippy::suspicious \
          -D clippy::style \
          -D clippy::complexity \
          -D clippy::perf \
          -D clippy::dbg_macro \
          -D clippy::todo \
          -D clippy::unimplemented \
          -D warnings

  unit-test:
    name: unit-test (${{ matrix.name }}${{ matrix.rust-special }})
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    continue-on-error: false
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false # cancel all jobs as soon as one fails?
      matrix:
        # Order this way because macOS typically has the longest duration, followed by Windows, so it benefits total workflow execution time.
        # Additionally, the 'linux (msrv *)' special case will then be listed next to the other 'linux' jobs.
        # Note: Windows uses '--target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc' by default as Cargo argument.
        include:
          - name: macos
            os: macos-latest # arm64

          - name: windows
            os: windows-latest

          # Don't use latest Ubuntu (22.04) as it breaks lots of ecosystem compatibility.
          # If ever moving to ubuntu-latest, need to manually install libtinfo5 for LLVM.
          - name: linux
            os: ubuntu-22.04

          - name: linux
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            rust-toolchain: nightly
            rust-special: -minimal-deps
            rust-cache-key: minimal-deps

          - name: linux
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            rust-toolchain: "1.87"
            rust-special: -msrv

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
        # Only on Linux because godot4-prebuilt/nightly branch doesn't have artifacts for other platforms.
        if: matrix.name == 'linux' && matrix.rust-special == ''
        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
        with:
          rust: ${{ matrix.rust-toolchain || 'stable' }}
          cache-key: ${{ matrix.rust-cache-key }} # only needed when rustc version is possibly the same

      # Set Cargo features also to minimal; detects when doctests refer to API symbols outside the minimal codegen set.
      - name: "Install minimal dependency versions from Cargo"
        if: matrix.rust-special == '-minimal-deps'
        run: |
          cargo +nightly update -Z minimal-versions
          echo "TEST_FEATURES=--no-default-features" >> $GITHUB_ENV

      - name: "Compile tests"
        run: cargo test $TEST_FEATURES --no-run ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}

      - name: "Test"
        run: cargo test $TEST_FEATURES ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}


  unit-test-web:
    name: unit-test-web (${{ matrix.name }})
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - name: Threads
            command: test-web-t
          - name: No threads
            command: test-web-nt

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Patch Cargo.toml to use nightly extension API"
        run: .github/other/patch-prebuilt.sh nightly

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
        with:
          rust: nightly
          components: rust-src

      - name: "Install emscripten"
        env:
          EMSCRIPTEN_SRC_REF: 4.0.13
          EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION: 3.1.74
        run: |
          git clone https://github.com/emscripten-core/emsdk.git --depth 1 --branch "$EMSCRIPTEN_SRC_REF" --single-branch
          ./emsdk/emsdk install "$EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION"
          ./emsdk/emsdk activate "$EMSCRIPTEN_VERSION"
          source ./emsdk/emsdk_env.sh
          echo "$PATH" >> "$GITHUB_PATH"

      - name: "Test (Wasm)"
        run: ./check.sh ${{ matrix.command }}


  miri-test:
    name: miri-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust
        with:
          rust: nightly
          components: miri

      - name: "Setup Miri"
        run: cargo miri setup

      - name: "Compile tests"
        run: cargo miri test -p godot-cell --no-run

      - name: "Test stacked borrows"
        run: cargo miri test -p godot-cell

      - name: "Test tree borrows"
        run: MIRIFLAGS="-Zmiri-tree-borrows" cargo miri test -p godot-cell

  proptest:
    name: proptest
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Install Rust"
        uses: ./.github/composite/rust

      - name: "Compile tests"
        run: cargo test -p godot-cell --features="proptest" --no-run

      - name: "Test"
        run: cargo test -p godot-cell --features="proptest"

  # For complex matrix workflow, see https://stackoverflow.com/a/65434401
  godot-itest:
    name: godot-itest (${{ matrix.name }})
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    continue-on-error: false
    timeout-minutes: 24
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false # cancel all jobs as soon as one fails?
      matrix:
        # Naming: {os}[-{runtimeVersion}]-{apiVersion}
        # runtimeVersion = version of Godot binary; apiVersion = version of GDExtension API against which gdext is compiled.

        # Config overview (see job for details).
        #
        # Cross-platform:
        # - Base version:     Godot nightly, no custom
        # - Double precision: Godot nightly, custom (bindgen), double
        # - 4.2 compat:       Godot 4.2
        #
        # Linux-only:
        # - Full:             Godot nightly, full codegen
        # - Double + lazy:    Godot nightly, custom, double, lazy func tables
        # - Features + exp:   Godot nightly, custom, threads, serde, experimental API
        # - Memcheck nightly: Godot mem nightly, custom, sanitizer
        # - Memcheck 4.x:     Godot mem 4.2, sanitizer

        # Note: Windows uses '--target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc' by default as Cargo argument.
        include:
          # macOS -- for the unintuitive naming, see https://github.com/actions/runner-images?tab=readme-ov-file#available-images

          - name: macos-arm
            os: macos-latest
            artifact-name: macos-arm-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64
            hot-reload: stable

           # api-custom on macOS arm64 not working, due to clang linker issues.
#          - name: macos-double-arm
#            os: macos-latest
#            artifact-name: macos-double-arm-nightly
#            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.double.arm64
#            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision

          # Lag one version behind latest release.
          - name: macos-arm-4.5
            os: macos-latest
            artifact-name: macos-arm-4.5
            godot-binary: godot.macos.editor.dev.arm64
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'

          # Windows

          - name: windows
            os: windows-latest
            artifact-name: windows-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.x86_64.exe
            hot-reload: stable

          - name: windows-double
            os: windows-latest
            artifact-name: windows-double-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.double.x86_64.exe
            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision

          # Lag one version behind latest release.
          - name: windows-4.5
            os: windows-latest
            artifact-name: windows-4.5
            godot-binary: godot.windows.editor.dev.x86_64.exe
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'

          # Linux

          # Don't use latest Ubuntu (22.04) as it breaks lots of ecosystem compatibility.
          # If ever moving to ubuntu-latest, need to manually install libtinfo5 for LLVM.

          # Uses full+experimental codegen, so that compatibility breakage towards nightly is detected.
          # If the experimental part causes problems, consider using only godot/__codegen-full.
          - name: linux-full
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            rust-extra-args: --features itest/codegen-full,itest/test-gdextension-dependency
            rust-extra-env: GODOT_RUST_MAIN_EXTENSION=IntegrationTests
            hot-reload: api-custom

          # Combines now a lot of features, but should be OK. lazy-function-tables doesn't work with experimental-threads.
          - name: linux-double-lazy
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-double-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.double.x86_64
            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom,godot/double-precision,itest/codegen-full,godot/lazy-function-tables

          - name: linux-features-experimental
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            # Important to keep both experimental-threads and codegen-full. Some itests (native_st_audio) require both.
            rust-extra-args: --features itest/experimental-threads,itest/codegen-full-experimental,godot/api-custom,godot/serde,itest/register-docs

          # Compiles godot-rust with `api-custom-json` feature against the JSON file generated via `--dump-extension-api`.
          # Uses latest 4.x headers, while `extension_api.json` comes from the latest Godot binary.
          - name: linux-custom-api-json
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom-json
            godot-indirect-json: true

          - name: linux-release
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-release-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.template_release.x86_64
            # Use `codegen-full-experimental` to make sure that all function tables can be loaded in Godot release builds.
            # If the experimental part causes problems, downgrade to `codegen-full`.
            rust-extra-args: --release --features itest/codegen-full-experimental
            rust-cache-key: release

          # Linux compat:
          # No hot-reload before 4.4, as the Godot project is 4.4+.

          - name: linux-4.6
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-4.6
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            #godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.6'
            hot-reload: stable

          - name: linux-4.5
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-4.5
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.5'
            hot-reload: api-4-5

          - name: linux-4.4
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-4.4
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.4'
            hot-reload: api-4-4

          - name: linux-4.3
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-4.3
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.3'
            hot-reload: api-4-3

          # Can be disabled in the future (already covered by memcheck). For now kept on to differentiate quicker.
          - name: linux-4.2
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-4.2
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.2'
            hot-reload: api-4-2

          # Memory checks: special Godot binaries compiled with AddressSanitizer/LeakSanitizer to detect UB/leaks. Always Linux.
          # See also https://rustc-dev-guide.rust-lang.org/sanitizers.html.
          #
          # Additionally, the Godot source is patched to make dlclose() a no-op, as unloading dynamic libraries loses stacktrace and
          # cause false positives like println!. See https://github.com/google/sanitizers/issues/89.
          #
          # There is also a gcc variant besides clang, which is currently not used.
          - name: memcheck-nightly
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
            rust-toolchain: nightly
            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
            rust-extra-args: --features godot/api-custom
            # Sanitizers can't build proc-macros and build scripts; with --target, cargo ignores RUSTFLAGS for those two.
            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

          - name: memcheck-release-disengaged
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
            rust-toolchain: nightly
            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
            # Currently without itest/codegen-full-experimental.
            rust-extra-args: --release --features godot/safeguards-release-disengaged
            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
            rust-target-dir: release # We're running Godot debug build with Rust release dylib.

          - name: memcheck-dev-balanced
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-nightly
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
            rust-toolchain: nightly
            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
            # Currently without itest/codegen-full-experimental.
            rust-extra-args: --features godot/safeguards-dev-balanced
            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

          - name: memcheck-4.2
            os: ubuntu-22.04
            artifact-name: linux-memcheck-4.2
            godot-binary: godot.linuxbsd.editor.dev.x86_64.llvm.san
            godot-prebuilt-patch: '4.2' # check compat of API 4.2 with newer binaries.
            rust-toolchain: nightly
            rust-env-rustflags: -Zrandomize-layout -Zsanitizer=address
            # Sanitizers can't build proc-macros and build scripts; with --target, cargo ignores RUSTFLAGS for those two.
            rust-target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Run Godot integration test"
        uses: ./.github/composite/godot-itest
        with:
          artifact-name: godot-${{ matrix.artifact-name }}
          godot-binary: ${{ matrix.godot-binary }}
          godot-args: ${{ matrix.godot-args }} # currently unused
          godot-prebuilt-patch: ${{ matrix.godot-prebuilt-patch }}
          rust-extra-args: ${{ matrix.rust-extra-args }}
          rust-toolchain: ${{ matrix.rust-toolchain || 'stable' }}
          rust-env-rustflags: ${{ matrix.rust-env-rustflags }}
          rust-extra-env: ${{ matrix.rust-extra-env }}
          rust-target: ${{ matrix.rust-target }}
          rust-target-dir: ${{ matrix.rust-target-dir }}
          rust-cache-key: ${{ matrix.rust-cache-key }}
          with-llvm: ${{ contains(matrix.name, 'macos') && contains(matrix.rust-extra-args, 'api-custom') }}
          godot-check-header: ${{ matrix.godot-check-header }}
          godot-indirect-json: ${{ matrix.godot-indirect-json }}

      - name: "Build and test hot-reload"
        if: ${{ matrix.hot-reload }}
        working-directory: itest/hot-reload/godot
        # Repeat a few times, our hot reload integration test can sometimes be a bit flaky.
        # Don't pass in rust-extra-args as environment; they're intended for itest.
        run: $RETRY ./run-test.sh ${{ matrix.hot-reload }}
        shell: bash


  cargo-deny-machete:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      # Deny
      # Note: manually downloading is ~30s faster than https://github.com/EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny-action
      - name: "Install cargo-deny"
        run: |
          wget --no-verbose https://github.com/EmbarkStudios/cargo-deny/releases/download/$CARGO_DENY_VERSION/cargo-deny-$CARGO_DENY_VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl.tar.gz -O cargo-deny.tar.gz
          tar -zxvf cargo-deny.tar.gz
          mkdir -p $HOME/.cargo/bin
          mv cargo-deny-$CARGO_DENY_VERSION-x86_64-unknown-linux-musl/cargo-deny $HOME/.cargo/bin
      - name: "Deny non-conforming dependencies"
        run: cargo deny check --config .github/other/deny.toml

      # Machete
      - name: "Install cargo-machete"
        uses: baptiste0928/cargo-install@v3
        with:
          crate: cargo-machete
          version: ${{ env.CARGO_MACHETE_VERSION }}
      - name: "Use machete to cut down dependencies"
        run: cargo machete


  license-guard:
    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: "Check license headers"
        uses: 'apache/skywalking-eyes/header@v0.6.0'
        with:
          # log: debug # optional: set the log level. The default value is `info`.
          config: .github/other/licenserc.yml
          # token: # optional: the token that license eye uses when it needs to comment on the pull request.
          # Set to empty ("") to disable commenting on pull request. The default value is ${{ github.token }}
          # mode: # optional: Which mode License-Eye should be run in. Choices are `check` or `fix`. The default value is `check`.
          mode: check


  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # CI status report

  # Job to notify merge queue about success/failure
  ci-status:
    # Check for 'merge_group' not strictly necessary, but helpful when adding add-hoc `push:` trigger to `on:` for testing branch.
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'merge_group'
    needs:
      - rustfmt
      - doc-lints
      - clippy
      - unit-test
      - miri-test
      - proptest
      - godot-itest
      - cargo-deny-machete
      - license-guard

    runs-on: ubuntu-22.04
    steps:
      # Deliberate choice to use bash script and not GitHub Action glob syntax, as that is not well-documented and hard to get right.
      # For example: contains(needs.*.result, 'success') does NOT work because * is a logical OR, thus true if a single job succeeds.
      - name: "Determine success or failure"
        run: |
          DEPENDENCIES='${{ toJson(needs) }}'

          echo "Dependency jobs:"
          all_success=true
          for job in $(echo "$DEPENDENCIES" | jq -r 'keys[]'); do
              status=$(echo "$DEPENDENCIES" | jq -r ".[\"$job\"].result")
              echo "* $job -> $status"
              if [[ "$status" != "success" ]]; then
                  all_success=false
              fi
          done

          if [[ "$all_success" == "false" ]]; then
              echo "One or more dependency jobs failed or were cancelled."
              exit 1
          fi

# Copyright (c) godot-rust; Bromeon and contributors.
# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

name: rust
description: "Install Rust toolchain, with caching"

inputs:
  rust:
    required: false
    description: "Rust toolchain, e.g. 'stable' or 'nightly'"
    default: stable

  components:
    required: false
    description: "Components array"
    default: ''

  cache-key:
    required: false
    description: "Extra key to resolve cache"
    default: ''

  with-llvm:
    required: false
    description: "Set to 'true' if LLVM should be installed"
    default: ''

runs:
  using: "composite"
  steps:
    - name: "Configure"
      id: configure
      env:
        CS: ${{ inputs.components }}
      # TODO use actual Rust version, e.g. 1.78 in cache, not just "stable"
      run: |
        echo "Rust cache shared-key: '${{ runner.os }}-${{ inputs.rust }}${{ inputs.cache-key }}'"
        echo "components=$( for c in ${CS//,/ }; do echo -n ' --component' $c; done )" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
      shell: bash

      # Spurious failures for rustup:
      #   info: update not yet available, sorry! try again later
      #   error: toolchain 'nightly-x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu' is not installable
    - name: "Rustup"
      run: |
        $RETRY rustup toolchain install ${{ inputs.rust }} --profile minimal --no-self-update ${{ steps.configure.outputs.components }}
        rustup default ${{ inputs.rust }}
      shell: bash

    - name: "Extract Rust version"
      # Transforms into format "1.78.0-stable" or "1.80.0-nightly-d2d24e395", which can be used as cache key.
      run: |
        version=$(rustc --version)
        if echo "$version" | grep -q "nightly"; then
          version=$(echo "$version" | sed -E 's/rustc ([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+)-nightly \(([a-f0-9]+) .*/\1-nightly-\2/')
        else
          version=$(echo "$version" | sed -E 's/rustc ([0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+) .*/\1-stable/')
        fi
        echo "INSTALLED_RUST_VERSION=$version" >> $GITHUB_ENV
      shell: bash

    - name: "Reuse cached dependencies"
      uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
      with:
        # A cache key that is used instead of the automatic `job`-based key, and is stable over multiple jobs.
        # default: empty
        shared-key: "${{ runner.os }}-${{ env.INSTALLED_RUST_VERSION }}${{ inputs.cache-key }}"

        # An additional cache key that is added alongside the automatic `job`-based
        # cache key and can be used to further differentiate jobs.
        # default: empty
        #key: ${{ inputs.cache-key }}

        # Determines if the cache should be saved even when the workflow has failed.
        # default: "false"
        cache-on-failure: true

        # The prefix cache key, this can be changed to start a new cache manually.
        # default: "v0-rust"
        #prefix-key: "v1-rust"

        # Determines which crates are cached.
        # If `true` all crates will be cached, otherwise only dependent crates will be cached.
        # Useful if additional crates are used for CI tooling.
        # default: "false"
        #cache-all-crates: true

    - name: "Install LLVM"
      if: inputs.with-llvm == 'true'
      uses: ./.github/composite/llvm

    - name: "Set environment variables used by toolchain"
      run: |
        echo CARGO_TERM_COLOR=always >> $GITHUB_ENV
        echo CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 >> $GITHUB_ENV
        echo RUST_BACKTRACE=1 >> $GITHUB_ENV
        
        rustc --version --verbose
      shell: bash

#!/bin/bash
# Copyright (c) godot-rust; Bromeon and contributors.
# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

# Sets up a patch in Cargo.toml to use godot4-prebuilt artifacts.
# Input: $version

version="$1"

# Add correct feature to `godot` dependency.
if [[ "$version" == "nightly" ]]; then
  # Do not use extraFeatures="api-custom" here. They just want to use nightly Godot with current API.
  extraFeatures=""
else
  # Extract "major.minor" from "major.minor[.patch]" -- disabled.
  #  dashedVersion=$(echo "$version" | cut -d '.' -f 1,2 | sed 's/\./-/')

  # Convert . to - for feature name.
  dashedVersion="${version//./-}"
  extraFeatures=", \"api-$dashedVersion\""
fi

# Add extra features to the godot dependency (expects existing `features` key).
# -i'' for macOS sed compat (BSD-based), see https://stackoverflow.com/a/14813278.
sed -i'' -e "/^godot = /s/\(features = \[\([^]]*\)\)\]/\1$extraFeatures]/g" itest/rust/Cargo.toml

echo "Patched Cargo.toml for version $version$extraFeatures:"
grep "features =" itest/rust/Cargo.toml

bindgen = { version = "0.72.1", default-features = false, features = ["runtime"] }
